items.title: ‘Eid does not enter tents’: Palestinians in Gaza face grim holiday
items.summary: Rising costs and displacement erase Eid traditions, leaving Gaza residents struggling with sorrow and economic hardship.

items.title: Israeli authorities filmed treating Palestinians with aggression on Eid
items.summary: Israeli authorities have been filmed acting aggressively against Palestinians under occupation during Eid al-Adha.

items.title: Šefica britanskih špijuna: Rusija nemilosrdno cilja našu infrastrukturu
items.summary: ŠEFICA GCHQ-a upozorava da Rusija cilja britansku infrastrukturu i demokraciju, dok se tehnološka prednost nad Kinom smanjuje, uvodeći UK u 'novu eru radikalne nesigurnosti'.

items.title: Woman caught after 30 years on the run is jailed for armed robberies
items.summary: Klette was finally caught in a flat in Berlin in 2024 and put on trial last year.

items.title: Hotel that refused to give tourist tap water acted lawfully, Italian court rules
items.summary: The Italian Supreme Court rejected a tourist's claim that her consumer rights were breached when she was only offered €7 bottled mineral water.

items.title: Ghana welcomes Pope's apology over Catholic Church's role in slavery
items.summary: The West African nation was one of the main gateways for the transatlantic slave trade.

items.title: UFC fighting cage rises on White House lawn for US and Trump celebrations
items.summary: The UFC bout at the White House marks the 250th anniversary of the US and President Donald Trump's 80th birthday.

items.title: Španjolski premijer nakon upada policije u sjedište stranke. "Ništa ne skrivamo"
items.summary: ŠPANJOLSKI premijer Pedro Sanchez rekao je da njegova Socijalistička stranka (PSOE), u čije je sjedište ušla policija u sklopu istrage oko mogućeg nezakonitog uzimanja javnog novca, "nište ne skriva", te je odbio zahtjev oporbene desnice o raspisivanju prijevremenih izbora.

items.title: Spain police enter PM Sanchez’s Socialist Party HQ in corruption probe
items.summary: Spanish police enter governing party's headquarters in Madrid as the corruption cases circle PM Sanchez's government.

items.title: Pochettino raises expectations as he reveals USMNT 2026 World Cup roster
items.summary: Instead of playing down his side's chances, Mauricio Pochettino implies that US can win the World Cup on home soil.

items.title: The West only discovers property rights when the landowners are white
items.summary: Zimbabwe is compensating European farmers. The original dispossession goes unrecognised.

items.title: Hoće li se ukinuti funkcija Visokog predstavnika u BiH?
items.summary: BIVŠI visoki predstavnici za BiH Carl Bildt i Wolfgang Petrich u zajedničkom su istupu u njemačkom dnevniku Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung (FAZ) zatražili da se ta funkcija ukine.

items.title: In Britain, Brexit is debated again as Starmer’s grip on power slips
items.summary: The PM is promising to strengthen EU ties at a time when the eurosceptic Reform party is on the rise.

items.title: Trump-backed challenger beats veteran US senator in Texas primary
items.summary: Ken Paxton's victory sets up what will be a closely watched contest in November's midterm elections.

items.title: Five people found alive after week trapped in flooded Laos cave
items.summary: The search is continuing for a further two villagers who are still missing, rescuers say.

items.title: VIDEO Petero ljudi nakon tjedan dana pronađeno živo u poplavljenoj špilji u Laosu
items.summary: SPASIOCI su u Laosu pronašli petero ljudi živih u poplavljenoj špilji tjedan dana nakon što su ostali zarobljeni. Dvoje se još vode kao nestali, a potraga za njima se nastavlja.

items.summary: Doctor buried after he died treating Ebola patients in DRC

items.summary: Ghana begins repatriating citizens as xenophobia surges in South Africa

items.title: Messi’s GOAT statue in India to be removed over safety concerns
items.summary: Statue to honour World Cup winner Lionel Messi, unveiled during his GOAT tour of India, unstable on busy Kolkata road.

items.title: How Senegal became genuine World Cup 2026 contenders – at a cost
items.summary: The West African nation's success comes from smart diaspora recruitment, as well as controversial local academies.
